title: Seed of the Woman
summary: The protoevangelium—first gospel promise—unfolds from Genesis 3:15 through the narrowing of Abraham's seed to Christ, the ultimate serpent-crusher.

links.note: Initial promise: seed of woman will crush serpent's head
Blessing narrowed to Abraham's offspring
Reaffirmed through Isaac
Paul identifies the singular 'seed' as Christ
Born of a woman, born under law

images.caption: The protoevangelium — seed of the woman
images.credit: Julius Schnorr von Carolsfeld · Public domain

title: Scepter from Judah
summary: Jacob's blessing prophesies that the royal scepter will not depart from Judah until Shiloh comes—a prophecy linking kingship to the Messiah through David's line.

links.note: Scepter prophecy: until Shiloh comes
Davidic covenant establishes eternal throne
God's king installed on Zion
Child born to reign on David's throne forever
Genealogy establishes Jesus as son of David, tribe of Judah
Angel declares Jesus will inherit David's throne

title: Prophet Like Moses
summary: Moses prophesies a future prophet whom God will raise up from Israel, speaking God's words directly—a role ultimately filled by Jesus as the new lawgiver.

links.note: Promise of a prophet like Moses
Jews ask John if he is 'the Prophet'; Philip identifies Jesus
Crowd recognizes Jesus as 'the Prophet'
Peter explicitly applies Deuteronomy to Jesus
Stephen quotes the prophecy in his defense

title: Davidic Covenant & Eternal Throne
summary: God's unconditional covenant with David promises an eternal dynasty, throne, and kingdom—progressively revealed as fulfilled in Christ's eternal reign.

links.note: Nathan delivers the covenant: house, throne, kingdom forever
Parallel account emphasizing eternal kingdom
Psalmist celebrates the covenant's permanence
God's oath to David
He will reign on David's throne forever
Branch of righteousness to reign
David my servant will be king over them forever
Gabriel announces Jesus will sit on David's throne forever
Peter declares Jesus is the promised descendant

images.caption: The Davidic covenant — an eternal throne
images.credit: Julius Schnorr von Carolsfeld · Public domain

title: The Suffering Servant
summary: Isaiah's four Servant Songs progressively reveal a figure who suffers vicariously for Israel and the nations—fulfilled in Jesus' passion and death.

links.note: First Servant Song: gentle justice-bringer
Second Servant Song: light to the nations
Third Servant Song: obedient despite suffering
Introduction to fourth song: exaltation after humiliation
Fourth Servant Song: vicarious suffering and death
Matthew quotes Isa 53:4 re: healing ministry
Matthew quotes Servant Song 1
Jesus quotes Isa 53:12—numbered with transgressors
Philip explains Isaiah 53 to Ethiopian eunuch
Paul quotes Isa 53:1 on Israel's unbelief

title: Born of a Virgin
summary: Isaiah's sign to Ahaz—a virgin conceiving Immanuel—finds its ultimate fulfillment in Mary's miraculous conception of Jesus.

links.note: The virgin shall conceive and bear a son, Immanuel
Immanuel connection continued
Matthew explicitly applies Isaiah 7:14 to Jesus
Gabriel announces virgin conception

title: Wonderful Counselor, Mighty God
summary: Isaiah's throne names reveal the divine nature and saving mission of the coming Davidic king—a child who is also Mighty God and Eternal Father.

links.note: Four-fold name and eternal Davidic reign
Jesus' kingdom will have no end
Savior, Christ the Lord born
The Word was God, became flesh
He is the image of the invisible God

title: Branch of Jesse
summary: The prophets use 'Branch' imagery to describe the coming righteous king from David's line who will restore justice and usher in an age of peace.

links.note: Shoot from Jesse's stump, Spirit-empowered righteous rule
Righteous Branch executing justice
Branch to spring up for David
My servant the Branch
The man called Branch—priest-king
He will be called a Nazarene (possible wordplay on netzer/branch)
Paul quotes Isaiah 11:10—root of Jesse

title: Ruler from Bethlehem
summary: Micah prophesies that from tiny Bethlehem will come a ruler whose origins are from ancient days—pointing to Christ's dual nature and birthplace.

links.note: Bethlehem Ephrathah—ruler whose origins are from of old
Scribes quote Micah to Herod; Jesus born in Bethlehem
Joseph travels to Bethlehem for census; Jesus born
Crowd debates whether Christ comes from Bethlehem

title: Humble King on a Donkey
summary: Zechariah prophesies a king entering Jerusalem humbly on a donkey—fulfilled precisely in Jesus' triumphal entry.

links.note: King comes gentle, riding on a donkey
Triumphal entry explicitly fulfills Zechariah
Jesus enters on colt
Blessed is the king who comes
John quotes Zechariah 9:9

title: The Pierced One
summary: Zechariah prophesies that Israel will look on the one they pierced and mourn—a text applied to Christ's crucifixion and future recognition.

links.note: They will look on me, the one they have pierced
Fountain for cleansing; shepherd struck
Jesus quotes 'strike the shepherd'
Soldier pierces Jesus' side; John cites Zechariah

title: Priest Forever After Melchizedek
summary: Psalm 110 declares the Messiah a priest forever in the order of Melchizedek—a pre-Aaronic priesthood that Hebrews expounds at length.

links.note: Melchizedek—priest of God Most High—blesses Abraham
You are a priest forever in the order of Melchizedek
Jesus uses Psalm 110 to show Messiah is David's Lord

title: Son of Man Coming with Clouds
summary: Daniel's vision of one like a son of man receiving eternal dominion shapes Jesus' self-designation and his claims before the Sanhedrin.

links.note: One like a son of man approaches the Ancient of Days
Son of Man coming on clouds with power
Jesus tells Caiaphas he will see Son of Man coming
Jesus affirms Daniel 7 imagery at trial
Stephen sees Son of Man at God's right hand

images.caption: Daniel's vision of the Son of Man

title: Seventy Weeks
summary: Daniel's prophecy of seventy weeks outlines a timeline culminating in the Anointed One being cut off—a framework for Messiah's coming and death.

links.note: Seventy 'sevens' decreed; Anointed One cut off
Ministry timing—15th year of Tiberius
The time is fulfilled, kingdom at hand
Fullness of time

title: Out of Egypt I Called My Son
summary: Hosea's reflection on Israel's exodus becomes a type fulfilled when Jesus returns from Egypt—Israel's story recapitulated in the Messiah.

links.note: Israel is my firstborn son
Out of Egypt I called my son
Flight to Egypt and return fulfills Hosea

title: Voice in the Wilderness
summary: Isaiah's call to prepare a way in the wilderness is applied by all four Gospels to John the Baptist as forerunner of Christ.

links.note: A voice crying: prepare the way of the Lord
I will send my messenger ahead of you
I will send Elijah before the day of the Lord
Matthew quotes Isaiah 40 re: John
Jesus identifies John as Elijah figure
Mark opens with Isaiah 40 and Malachi
Zechariah prophesies John's role
Luke quotes Isaiah 40 at length
John identifies himself with Isaiah 40:3

title: Anointed to Preach Good News
summary: Isaiah 61's Spirit-anointed herald of jubilee is read by Jesus in Nazareth and declared fulfilled in their hearing—his mission statement.

links.note: Spirit of the Lord upon me to proclaim good news
Jesus reads Isaiah 61 in Nazareth, declares it fulfilled

title: My God, Why Have You Forsaken Me?
summary: Psalm 22 details the sufferings of David's greater Son—pierced hands, mocking crowds, garments divided—all fulfilled at the cross.

links.note: Cry of abandonment
Mocking, shaking heads
Bones out of joint, pierced hands and feet, casting lots
Casting lots, mocking, 'Eli, Eli'
Dividing garments, shaking heads, cry of dereliction
Soldiers cast lots for tunic; 'I thirst'

title: You Will Not Let Your Holy One See Decay
summary: The hope of resurrection emerges gradually in Israel's Scripture — from the Psalter's trust that God will not abandon the faithful to Sheol, through the prophets' visions of death swallowed up, to the explicit expectations in Daniel. The NT authors read these texts as fulfilled in Jesus' rising.

links.note: My Redeemer lives — the earliest personal hope of vindication beyond death
You will not abandon me to the grave, nor let your faithful one see decay
God will redeem me from the realm of the dead; he will surely take me to himself
He will swallow up death forever — the prophetic vision of death's abolition
Your dead will live; their bodies will rise — the clearest OT resurrection promise
On the third day he will raise us up — the 'third day' motif echoed in the kerygma
Many who sleep in the dust will awake — individual bodily resurrection made explicit
Peter at Pentecost: Psalm 16 was fulfilled when God raised Jesus from the dead
Paul at Antioch: the one God raised did not see decay — Psalm 16 realized

title: The New Covenant
summary: Jeremiah's promise of a new covenant written on hearts—fulfilled in Christ's blood and the Spirit's ministry.

links.note: New covenant: law on hearts, sins forgiven
New heart, new spirit, Spirit within
This is my blood of the covenant
Blood of the covenant poured out
This cup is the new covenant in my blood
Ministers of new covenant, Spirit gives life

title: Abrahamic Covenant: Blessing to All Nations
summary: God's promise to Abraham—land, seed, blessing—unfolds through Genesis and finds fulfillment in Christ as the blessing extends to all nations.

links.note: Initial promise: great nation, blessing to all peoples
Covenant ceremony, land promise, faith credited as righteousness
Covenant sign (circumcision), name change, everlasting covenant
Sworn promise after Isaac's near-sacrifice
Abraham father of all who believe
Gentiles blessed with Abraham; believers are Abraham's offspring

images.caption: God's covenant with Abraham
images.credit: Julius Schnorr von Carolsfeld · Public domain

title: Mosaic Covenant: Law and Tabernacle
summary: The Sinai covenant establishes Israel as God's treasured possession through law and sacrificial system—shadows pointing to Christ.

links.note: Kingdom of priests, holy nation
Ten Commandments
Covenant ratified with blood
Build a sanctuary according to pattern
Christ is the end of the law for righteousness
Law as guardian until Christ came
These are a shadow; the reality is Christ

title: Noahic Covenant: Common Grace
summary: God's post-flood covenant with all creation preserves the natural order and foreshadows final restoration—never again destruction by flood.

links.note: Seedtime and harvest, never again curse ground
Everlasting covenant with all life, rainbow sign
As I swore in days of Noah

title: The Day of the Lord
summary: The prophets repeatedly warn of 'the day of the Lord'—a day of darkness and judgment that finds partial fulfillment in historical events and ultimate fulfillment in Christ's return.

links.note: Day against the proud and lofty
Day of the Lord near—destruction from Almighty
Day of the Lord is near
Day of darkness; sun to darkness, moon to blood
Woe to those desiring the day—darkness not light
Great day of the Lord near—day of wrath
Burning like furnace; Elijah before the day
Peter quotes Joel at Pentecost
Day of Lord comes like a thief
Day will not come until...

images.caption: The Day of the Lord — judgment and redemption

title: Exile and Return
summary: Moses predicted exile for covenant unfaithfulness; the prophets specify Babylon as the agent; then promise restoration after 70 years.

links.note: Exile curses for disobedience
Restoration promise after exile
Babylon will carry everything away
70 years serving Babylon
After 70 years, I will bring you back
Daniel reads Jeremiah's 70 years
Cyrus fulfills Jeremiah's word

title: Fall of the Northern Kingdom
summary: The prophets warned Israel (northern kingdom) of Assyrian conquest for her idolatry—fulfilled in 722 BC.

links.note: God will strike Israel, uproot
Assyria captures Samaria—covenant violations listed
End of house of Israel
Return to Egypt, wanderers among nations
Exile beyond Damascus
Israel will surely go into exile

title: Oracles Against the Nations
summary: The prophets pronounce judgment on surrounding nations—Babylon, Edom, Tyre, Egypt, Assyria—demonstrating God's sovereignty over all history.

links.note: Oracle against Babylon
Oracle against Damascus
Oracle against Egypt
Babylon captured—images shamed
Against Edom
Against Tyre
Oracle against Nineveh
Against Edom

title: Fall of Babylon
summary: The prophets predicted Babylon's fall to the Medes and Persians—fulfilled in 539 BC when Cyrus captured the city.

links.note: Medes against Babylon—never inhabited again
Babylon has fallen, has fallen
Cyrus named as God's shepherd
Cyrus anointed to subdue nations
Kings of the Medes against Babylon
Handwriting on wall; Babylon falls that night

title: Four Kingdoms and God's Eternal Kingdom
summary: Daniel's visions reveal a sequence of world empires—Babylon, Persia, Greece, Rome—ultimately replaced by God's indestructible kingdom.

links.note: Statue of four metals; stone strikes
Four beasts; Son of Man receives kingdom
Ram (Persia), goat (Greece) named
Augustus (Roman emperor) context of Jesus' birth
Kingdom of God is at hand

title: Regathering of Israel
summary: The prophets promise Israel's regathering from exile—partially fulfilled in the post-exilic return, with fuller spiritual dimensions in the church.

links.note: God will gather you from all nations
Second time to recover remnant
Bring my sons from afar
I will gather you from all nations
He who scattered will gather
Gather from nations, one nation in land
Cyrus releases exiles to return

title: New Heart and Spirit
summary: Ezekiel promises internal transformation—a new heart replacing stone, God's Spirit within—fulfilled through regeneration in Christ.

links.note: One heart, new spirit, heart of flesh
Clean water, new heart, my Spirit within you
Born of water and Spirit
Spirit of God lives in you
Written on hearts by Spirit
Washing of rebirth and renewal by Spirit

title: Valley of Dry Bones
summary: Ezekiel's vision of dry bones coming to life prophesies Israel's national restoration—and typifies spiritual resurrection through the Spirit.

links.note: Valley of bones brought to life
Walk in newness of life
Dead in trespasses, made alive with Christ
Dead in sins, God made alive

title: Temple Rebuilding
summary: The prophets promise temple restoration after exile—fulfilled in Zerubbabel's temple, with greater glory anticipated in Christ.

links.note: Cyrus shall say of Jerusalem, 'Let it be rebuilt'
City rebuilt on its ruins
Vision of new temple
Glory of this house greater than former
Zerubbabel's hands have laid foundation
Branch will build temple of the Lord
Temple completed
Jesus speaks of temple of his body

title: Spirit Poured Out on All Flesh
summary: Joel prophesies the outpouring of God's Spirit on all people—sons and daughters, old and young—fulfilled at Pentecost.

links.note: Pour out Spirit; sons and daughters prophesy
Spirit poured from on high
Pour out Spirit on offspring
I will pour out my Spirit on Israel
Pentecost: Peter quotes Joel 2
Spirit falls on Gentiles too

title: Adam as Type of Christ
summary: Paul explicitly identifies Adam as a 'type' of Christ—the first Adam bringing death, the last Adam bringing life and righteousness.

links.note: Adam created in God's image, given dominion
God breathed into Adam life
Death through Adam's sin
Adam a pattern of the one to come
Contrast: trespass vs gift
Last Adam a life-giving spirit

title: Isaac and the Substitute Sacrifice
summary: Abraham's near-sacrifice of Isaac on Moriah prefigures the Father offering His Son, with a substitute ram foreshadowing Christ the Lamb.

links.note: Take your son, your only son, whom you love
God himself will provide the lamb
Ram provided as substitute—YHWH Yireh
Behold, the Lamb of God
God gave his only Son
Did not spare his own Son

title: Joseph as Type of Christ
summary: Joseph's suffering, rejection by brothers, exaltation, and saving of his family prefigures Christ's pattern of humiliation followed by glorification.

links.note: Brothers hate him, sell him
Imprisoned though innocent
Exalted to second in Egypt
You meant evil; God meant it for good
God intended it for good—to save many lives
Stephen recounts Joseph's suffering and exaltation
Humbled himself, therefore God exalted him

title: Moses as Deliverer
summary: Moses' role as prophet, mediator, and deliverer from bondage typifies Christ, the greater Moses who delivers from sin and death.

links.note: Moses saved from death as infant
Sent to deliver Israel
Leads through Red Sea
Prophet like me
Jesus saved from Herod, called from Egypt
Law through Moses; grace and truth through Jesus
Stephen: Moses as type, prophet to come

title: Passover Lamb
summary: The Passover lamb whose blood protected Israel from death typifies Christ, our Passover sacrificed for us.

links.note: Lamb without defect, blood on doorposts
Blood as sign; death passes over
No bone broken
Lamb of God who takes away sin
Crucified at Passover preparation; no bones broken
Christ, our Passover lamb, has been sacrificed

title: Tabernacle and Temple as Shadow
summary: The tabernacle and temple—God's dwelling among Israel—serve as copies pointing to Christ, the true dwelling of God with humanity.

links.note: Make a sanctuary that I may dwell among them
Glory fills the tabernacle
Glory fills Solomon's temple
Word became flesh and tabernacled among us
Temple of his body
Shadow; reality belongs to Christ
We are being built into a dwelling for God

title: Manna and Bread from Heaven
summary: The manna that sustained Israel in the wilderness typifies Christ, the true bread from heaven who gives eternal life.

links.note: Bread from heaven; what is it?
Ate manna forty years
Man does not live by bread alone
True bread from heaven; I am the bread of life
Bread that gives life to the world

title: Bronze Serpent Lifted Up
summary: Moses' bronze serpent, lifted up for Israel's healing, is explicitly applied by Jesus to his own crucifixion—look and live.

links.note: Fiery serpents; bronze serpent on pole
As Moses lifted up serpent, so Son of Man must be lifted up
When I am lifted up, I will draw all people

title: Sign of Jonah
summary: Jesus identifies Jonah's three days in the fish as prefiguring his death, burial, and resurrection—the only sign given to his generation.

links.note: Jonah in belly of fish three days and nights
Fish vomits Jonah onto dry land
Sign of Jonah; three days in heart of earth
No sign except sign of Jonah
Son of Man will be a sign like Jonah

title: David as Shepherd-King
summary: David's role as shepherd of Israel prefigures Christ, the Good Shepherd who lays down his life for the sheep.

links.note: David taken from sheep to be anointed king
You will shepherd my people Israel
The Lord is my shepherd
My servant David will shepherd them
Ruler who will shepherd my people
I am the good shepherd; I lay down my life

title: Day of Atonement
summary: The Day of Atonement ritual—high priest entering the Most Holy Place with blood—typifies Christ's once-for-all entrance into heaven.

links.note: Aaron enters Most Holy Place once a year
Blood sprinkled on mercy seat
Scapegoat bears sins away
God presented Christ as sacrifice of atonement
Making peace through blood of cross

title: Sabbath Rest
summary: The Sabbath rest instituted at creation and codified at Sinai points forward to the rest offered in Christ and the eternal rest to come.

links.note: God rested on seventh day, blessed it
Remember the Sabbath; creation pattern
Sabbath linked to exodus redemption
Come to me, I will give you rest
Sabbath a shadow; reality is Christ

title: Rock in the Wilderness
summary: The rock struck by Moses that gave Israel water typifies Christ, the spiritual rock who provides living water.

links.note: Strike the rock at Horeb; water flows
Speak to rock; Moses strikes it
He split rocks in the wilderness
Living water I give—spring of eternal life
Rivers of living water from within
That rock was Christ

title: Cities of Refuge
summary: The cities of refuge that protected the innocent manslayer typify Christ, our refuge from judgment and condemnation.

links.note: Six cities of refuge designated
Cities accessible, manslayer may flee
Cities established as refuge
God is our refuge and strength
No condemnation for those in Christ Jesus

title: Firstfruits Offering
summary: The offering of firstfruits at harvest prefigures Christ's resurrection as the firstfruits of those who will rise.

links.note: Wave sheaf of firstfruits before the Lord
Bring firstfruits to house of the Lord
Christ has been raised, firstfruits of those who sleep

title: Kinsman-Redeemer
summary: The kinsman-redeemer institution—family member who redeems property and family—typifies Christ who became flesh to redeem his people.

links.note: Kinsman may redeem property or person
Boaz is one of our kinsman-redeemers
Spread your garment—you are redeemer
Boaz redeems Ruth and Naomi
Word became flesh—kinsman
Born of woman to redeem
In him we have redemption
